Mexico enters as the model's clear favorite, projected to win at 62%, while Czech Republic holds an 11% chance of victory. The substantial gap reflects a marked asymmetry in the statistical outlook between the two sides.

The draw carries meaningful weight at 26%, indicating a plausible outcome despite Mexico's advantage. The win probabilities do not overlap heavily, suggesting the model sees Mexico as the likely victor, but the one-in-four draw probability signals that a stalemate remains a competitive possibility rather than a remote scenario.

No sportsbook consensus is available yet, so direct comparison between model and market cannot be made at this stage.
